questions.reasoning_head: To determine the number of ways to choose 6 different numbers from the set \(\{1, 2, \ldots, 49\}\) such that at least two of the chosen numbers are consecutive, we can use the principle of complementary counting. This involves first calculating the total number of ways to choose 6 numbers from 49 and then subtracting the number of ways to choose 6 numbers such that no two numbers are consecutive.

\[
\int_{0}^{2} dx \int_{0}^{3} \left(x^2 + 2xy\right) dy
\]

First, we integrate with respect to \(y\):
\[
\int_{0}^{3} \left(x^2 + 2xy\right) dy = \int_{0}^{3} x^2 dy + \int_{0}^{3} 2xy dy
\]
\[
= x^2 \int_{0}^{3} dy + 2x \int_{0}^{3} y dy
\]
\[
= x^2 [y]_{0}^{3} + 2x \left[\frac{y^2}{2}\right]_{0}^{3}
\]
